classification_definitions.oracle_executable: Executed by the independent oracle through a registered action or value path.
classification_definitions.oracle_command: Executed by the independent oracle through the normalized find command.
classification_definitions.oracle_primitive: Primitive is executable, but its complete command/operator path is not.
classification_definitions.oracle_boundary: Compact below/at/above limit boundary is executable; material subsystem enforcement is not implied.
classification_definitions.oracle_registry: Canonical error metadata is executable synthetically; future subsystem fault injection is not implied.
classification_definitions.contract_only: Normative semantics are accepted, but successful execution is not implemented.
classification_definitions.explicitly_unsupported_v1: Native v1 rejects this behavior and must not reinterpret it.
classification_definitions.deferred_post_v1: Outside v1 and unavailable until a later versioned contract and gate.
classification_definitions.differential_exact: The one committed fixture produced equal normalized results.
classification_definitions.differential_different: The one committed fixture produced an expected, documented difference.
classification_definitions.adapter_rewrite: A proposed rewrite produced equal fixture results; no adapter or general applicability claim exists.

mongodb_experimental_cases.note: Both profiles require every requested scalar to occur as an immediate array element.
Explicit immediate-element equality has the same match set.
MongoDB scalar equality implicitly matches array elements; native equality compares the whole array.
An adapter can translate the proven all-array dataset subset to explicit element matching.
Both profiles count immediate dense array elements.
MongoDB also matches an immediate nested array element equal to the query array; native equality compares only the whole field value.
Both profiles distinguish absence with an explicit exists predicate.
Explicit null remains present in both profiles.
MongoDB null equality includes missing fields; native null equality does not.
MongoDB type-null is the explicit-null-only reference for native equality.
Int32, int64, exact double, and decimal one share numeric equality.
Native mapping equality ignores field presentation order; MongoDB embedded-document equality preserves it.
One-level dotted traversal over object array elements has the same positive-candidate result.
Inclusion projection can suppress _id while preserving explicit query order.
The explicit unique _id tie key makes both result sequences deterministic.
Simple binary string equality preserves distinct Unicode normalization forms.
